Heads up on non-gating CI pipeline check 'IDE code review analysis'

As you are aware, "IDE Code Review" was enabled in NX in late January 2026. To push 'AI Assisted IDE Code Review' adoption [As per Arun's ask], we are introducing a non-gating check that verifies whether an IDE code review has been completed prior to opening a Pull Request (PR) and check if one or more AI comments have been addressed.

How the check works:
• If the IDE code review has not been completed: The developer will be notified to perform the review and will be provided with a link to the necessary documentation and steps.
• If the IDE code review has been completed: The system will analyze the PR to verify that one or more comments have been addressed.

This data will be collected for metrics and reporting purposes.

Sample PRs for reference:

https://gh-nx.scm.engit.cisco.com/nx/nx/pull/81835/checks?check_run_id=7777492 - Preetham's PR [IDE Code Review done]  https://gh-nx.scm.engit.cisco.com/nx/nx/pull/63868/checks?check_run_id=7777406 - Anupma's test PR [IDE code review not done]

Important Note:
• Transition to Gating: In couple of weeks, the "IDE Code Review Analysis" check will become a gating requirement. To drive adoption, PRs will be blocked from merging unless the IDE code review has been successfully completed.
This is non-gating check. This is to check If IDE code review done before the PR or not

For SA on a file - the basic pre-requisite is that the compilation commands corresponding to the file are recorded and available in preset files in the workspace. If the compilation commands are missing, Static Analysis on the file is not possible.
The compilation commands are generally recorded as part of builds.

Typical reasons for files not being analysed:

The corresponding compilation commands are not recorded during the build and hence not available.
The compilation commands recorded result in a failure
Test files - that are not built as part of the platform builds and hence their compilation commands dont get recorded.

**What should be done for files which are not analysed? **
In general, files are not analysed because they have not been compiled in workspace with any platform builds.
For XR, files are analysed only for the latest built platform so files which are compiled for platforms earlier to latest build will not be analysed along with the files which are not at all compiled in the workspace.
Try building a platform which will compile such files and execute "run_sa" again. For XR, it is recommended to execute run_sa after each platform build.
If you are sure that the file is compiled with the platform but still not analysed with run_sa, please raise a case with

Requirement from legal around self-attestation for AI/copilot generated code. See legal guidelines here.
One of the four attestations below must be included in the PR Description.  PR description template already has the following list.

GenAI Attestation Compliance Requirement:
	•	GenAI-Compliance: NO_GENAI - GAI was not used (or, no additional notation is required)
	•	GenAI-Compliance: ALL_GENAI - The entirety of the code in this PR was generated by GAI alone
	•	GenAI-Compliance: REFINED_BY_USER - GAI was used to help create a draft that was subsequently customized or modified
	•	GenAI-Compliance: REFINED_BY_GENAI - Coder created a draft manually that was non-substantively modified by GAI (e.g., refactoring was performed by GAI on manually written code)

Not sure what you mean.  You mean it doesn't have to generate the make commands?
Because is uses existing cached compiler line (check under build/pctmp), if you change module.mk, arch specific flags in central location then cache is out of date. FYI

Today on "Stupid Compiler Tricks": How to get the errors in your files immediately instead of waiting for a whole module build.

Simply run:
/auto/ses/bin/run_sa -os nx -show_parent -file <YOUR_CHANGED_FILENAME>

Instead of whatever you were doing with gmake or bat!

As a side benefit, you also won't have to scroll through 50 pages of make commands. You'll just get a list of errors directly above you. And when those errors go through, you'll also get any static analysis issues.

Why it's so much faster, I have no idea. But it sure is.

PR Merge
If you've signed up for auto-commit via the 'commit:auto' label, then the merge will be automatically triggered once all the criterias are met. Otherwise, you may press the Run button to trigger the merge manually.

By default, the pull request's commits will be squashed into one commit and merged. You may opt-in to preserving the individual commits by adding the 'nosquash' label to this PR.

Refer to the Labels section of the conversation view of this PR to view or edit the applied labels.

Proposed Change:

Default Email Settings:
•              Disable by Default: The following notifications will be disabled by default:

       Initiated

       Withdrawn

       Approved

       Unapproved

       Rejected

       Expiry Warning

       Expired

       Committed

Customization Option:
•              User Control: Introduce an option in the CAT GUI  that allows users to enable email notifications for specific events according to their preferences.

Continued Email Notifications:
1.	Force Override Request Submitted
2.	Approval Request OVERRIDE_INFO_REQUEST - Additional Info Requested

This structured approach should help streamline the notification process while giving users control over the types of communications they receive.
